literal: to perform or operate at full capacity or in an optimal, productive manner
to ruin someone's plans, prospects, or chances of success
to falsify or manipulate financial records or accounts deliberately
to prepare a large quantity of food with vigor and enthusiasm
a person regarded as fashionable, relaxed, self-confident, and socially admired
a calm, composed, and level-headed mental state
to be made to wait or be kept waiting
a very long period of time
to become aware or realize something
to receive a blow or physical strike
to avoid responsibility, duty, or commitment
to acquire enough of a commodity or sufficient control of supply to dominate pricing or manipulate the market
the hierarchical structure of positions within a corporation or large organization
a derogatory term for a person, company, or organization perceived to receive government subsidies while contributing little
to be extremely expensive or to require a very large amount of money
to take a liking to someone or something
a person who spends a great deal of time sitting or lying on a couch
used hyperbolically to say that someone is so unpopular they would fail to be elected even to the lowest public office
extremely dissimilar; as different from each other as possible
to have no interest in or concern for something
